text.title: Micro MVPs Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Micro MVPs application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Micro-MVP MUST test ONE falsifiable hypothesis with a numeric prediction (e.g. '10/100 visitors will pay').
rule.rationale: Multi-hypothesis tests cannot be falsified per claim.

rule.statement: Build window MUST be ≤7 calendar days; over-cap is rejected as not-micro.
rule.rationale: Beyond 7 days the test slips into small-product mode and the original hypothesis drifts.

rule.statement: Manual / fake / Wizard-of-Oz back-end MUST be explicitly allowed AND named in the spec; hidden manual back-end is rejected.

rationale.source: Lean Startup — Wizard of Oz patterns

rule.rationale: Naming the manual back-end keeps the test honest; hiding it accumulates technical debt.

rule.statement: Gate event MUST be instrumented BEFORE launch; un-instrumented gates are rejected.
rule.rationale: Without instrumentation the gate fires invisibly.

rule.statement: At T+window-close, decision MUST be {go, kill, iterate} with cited gate counts; open-ended 'monitor' rejected.
rule.rationale: Open-ended monitors collapse to keep-the-test-alive forever.
